plain_definition: A list MAS publishes and maintains of persons who may be wrongly perceived as licensed, authorised, or regulated by MAS, or as having made an offer of a fund, investment or document that is registered with or recognised by MAS when it is not. Its purpose is early public warning: dealing with a person on the list means a consumer does not have the protections that apply under laws MAS administers. In the digital-asset context, MAS's announced single-currency stablecoin (SCS) framework would use placement on the Investor Alert List as one consequence of falsely claiming a token is 'MAS-regulated' (see the mas-regulated-stablecoin entry).
formal_definition: MAS media release, 'MAS Finalises Stablecoin Regulatory Framework' (15 August 2023) -- confirmed by direct fetch of the genuine PDF via the Singapore Government's press-release repository (sgpc.gov.sg) -- references Investor Alert List placement as a consequence of misrepresenting a token as MAS-regulated. This entry could not directly fetch MAS's own Investor Alert List page (mas.gov.sg/investor-alert-list): it returned MAS's now-familiar bot-blocked 'service ... currently unavailable' page on a live attempt made while drafting this entry (2026-07-13). The general description of the list's scope and purpose given in plain_definition therefore rests on search-engine-indexed summaries of MAS's own page content, not on this project's own direct reading of that page's text, and should be independently re-verified once mas.gov.sg is reachable.
